checks.message: All required top-level fields present: project, services, databases, entities, endpoints, workers, integrations, security, observability, meta, envVars, files
project.deploymentTarget 'cloudflare-workers' is a valid enum value
project.framework 'hono' is a valid enum value
API service declares healthCheck.live=/health and healthCheck.ready=/health. Workers do not expose traditional ports — single /health endpoint is correct for this runtime.
2 workers declared: queue-consumer (trigger: cf-queue-batch, queue: click-events) and cron-aggregator (trigger: cron:0 * * * *). Both consistent with D1 database declared in databases[].
All integration envVars declared: Upstash (UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN). Both appear in envVars.api[].
databases[0].migrationCommand='wrangler d1 migrations apply snaplink-db --remote' — correct for Cloudflare D1 deployment.

security.securityMdIncluded=true, security.secretScanningEnabled=true, security.corsWhitelistConfigured=true, security.rateLimitingStrategy='upstash-sliding-window' — Rule 13 baseline met. Rate limiting uses Upstash REST API (correct for CF Workers — no TCP Redis available).
security.authStrategy='jwt-hs256', security.passwordHashing='argon2id' — compliant with security.md. argon2id is Wasm-compatible in CF Workers via @node-rs/argon2.
security.piiFieldsEncrypted=false; security.notes confirms raw IP not stored — only CF-IPCountry country code in ClickEvent. Privacy-by-design for analytics without PHI scope.
No PHI entity names detected. project.complianceScope='none' — no HIPAA/PCI requirements. Entities: User, ShortLink, ClickEvent, AnalyticsSummary — standard consumer data.
observability.healthEndpointsPlanned=true; healthLive='/health' and healthReady='/health'. Single endpoint is correct for CF Workers (no traditional process health distinction).
observability.structuredLogging=true, correlationId=true via X-Request-Id middleware. Cloudflare Workers logs are streamed to Cloudflare Logpush for structured access.

services[] includes 3 Workers (api, queue-consumer, cron-aggregator). observability.distributedTracing=false — acceptable for this deployment. All Workers run on the same Cloudflare infrastructure; Cloudflare Trace Analytics covers cross-worker visibility. distributedTracingNote acknowledges deferral with a concrete plan (Cloudflare Analytics).

assumptions: KV eventual consistency (~60s propagation) accepted for redirect cache — stale redirect window is a product trade-off, not a security issue
CF Queue at-least-once delivery: duplicate ClickEvent rows are acceptable for analytics; no deduplication required
D1 SQLite used instead of PostgreSQL — standard SQL subset only; no ARRAY, JSON_AGG, or pg-specific functions used in blueprint
Upstash REST API used for rate limiting instead of TCP Redis — required for CF Workers compatibility; confirmed in integrations[]
Guest links expire after 30 days — enforced by Cron Trigger daily cleanup (WHERE is_guest=1 AND expires_at < unixepoch())
argon2id via @node-rs/argon2 (Wasm) — compatible with CF Workers; bcrypt not used (CPU-bound, exceeds Workers CPU limit)
